title: Phaseek: A Generalizable Predictor of Liquid–Liquid Phase Separation from Protein Sequence
author: Amir Mohammad Mohammad Hosseini
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This dataset highlights both random and key regions associated with phase separation across the proteomes of 18 different organisms, as analyzed by the Phaseek model. 
 The organisms and their corresponding abbreviations are: 
 
 
  Hs  –  Homo sapiens  
 
 
  Mm  –  Mus musculus  
 
 
  Rn  –  Rattus norvegicus  
 
 
  Dm  –  Drosophila melanogaster  
 
 
  Dr  –  Danio rerio  
 
 
  At  –  Arabidopsis thaliana  
 
 
  Sc  –  Saccharomyces cerevisiae  
 
 
  Ce  –  Caenorhabditis elegans  
 
 
  Bt  –  Bos taurus  
 
 
  Mmu  –  Macaca mulatta  
 
 
  Cf  –  Canis lupus familiaris  
 
 
  Ss  –  Sus scrofa  
 
 
  Gg  –  Gallus gallus  
 
 
  Xl  –  Xenopus laevis  
 
 
  EcK12  –  Escherichia coli  K-12 
 
 
  Pt  –  Pan troglodytes  
 
 
  Ag  –  Anopheles gambiae  
 
 
  EcSakai  –  Escherichia coli  Sakai 
 
 
  Pf  –  Plasmodium falciparum  
 
 
 The predictions were generated using  Phaseek , our deep learning model designed to identify sequence features associated with LLPS behavior. 
 This dataset was generated using the  Phaseek  predictor and is provided to support the findings presented in our paper  Generalizable prediction of liquid-liquid phase separation from protein sequence ( http://dx.doi.org/10.1101/2025.01.27.635039 )
